refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the Vehicle to complete the load→travel→dump cycle with a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement clarifies that cycle time is a measurable outcome dependent on vehicle performance, operational conditions, and safety constraints. The p95 metric and duty cycle context are essential to avoid over-specification or under-specification of performance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle conditions, with telemetry capture of cycle time components (load, travel, dump phases). Statistical analysis of p95 across multiple runs. Comparison against CT_target baseline.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The Vehicle shall execute load phase within load_time_budget
FR-2.2: The Vehicle shall execute travel phase within travel_time_budget
FR-2.3: The Vehicle shall execute dump phase within dump_time_budget
FR-2.4: The Teleoperation system shall not introduce latency exceeding latency_budget during remote operation
FR-2.5: The Safety Services shall not impose cycle time penalties exceeding safety_overhead_budget

candidate_dps: DP-2.1: Optimize vehicle drivetrain, hydraulics, and control algorithms to minimize phase execution times; allocate time budgets to load, travel, and dump subsystems
DP-2.2: Implement predictive telemetry and real-time cycle time monitoring with operator feedback to enable adaptive operation within time targets
DP-2.3: Design Safety Services to operate asynchronously and non-blocking to cycle time critical paths; use safety interlocks only where mandatory
DP-2.4: Establish duty cycle baseline and site condition characterization; validate cycle time performance under representative conditions before field deployment
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2: Decompose cycle time into phase budgets (load, travel, dump); optimize Vehicle subsystems (drivetrain, hydraulics, controls) to meet phase budgets; implement non-blocking Safety Services; establish telemetry-driven monitoring and adaptive operator feedback; validate against duty cycle baseline and site conditions.

potential_sources: Safety Services may couple to cycle time if interlocks are not carefully designed to avoid blocking critical paths
Teleoperation latency may couple to travel phase if network conditions are not characterized
Site conditions (terrain, load variability) may couple to phase budgets if not accounted for in baseline
coupling_check.notes: Coupling risks are manageable through explicit phase budget allocation, asynchronous safety design, and duty cycle characterization. No fundamental design contradiction identified.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle subsystems (drivetrain, hydraulics) can be optimized to meet allocated phase budgets without major redesign.
Assumption: Safety Services can be implemented asynchronously without compromising safety criticality.
Risk: Site conditions (terrain, load variability) may exceed baseline assumptions, causing p95 to exceed CT_target.
Risk: Teleoperation latency may be unpredictable in field conditions, impacting travel phase performance.
Risk: Operator skill and worksite policies may introduce variability not captured in controlled testing.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-2 to explicitly include contingency margins (e.g., 10-15% buffer) in phase budgets to account for site variability and operator skill variance. Add requirement for real-time cycle time telemetry and operator alerting when approaching time budget limits.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: The Vehicle module owns the mechanical, hydraulic, electrical, and control systems responsible for executing load, travel, and dump phases. It receives Tasking commands and produces Telemetry (cycle time, phase durations, operational state). It interfaces with Safety Services via SafetyAlert for safety-critical interlocks.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands from operator or Teleoperation system specifying load location, travel destination, and dump site. Includes duty cycle context and time budget expectations.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time and post-cycle telemetry including phase durations (load_time, travel_time, dump_time), cycle_time, operational state, and performance metrics for cycle time monitoring and optimization.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services communicate safety constraints and interlocks to Vehicle (e.g., speed limits, geofence boundaries). Vehicle reports safety-relevant operational state (e.g., load secured, operator present).

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for Teleoperation system to send Tasking and receive Telemetry. Latency on this link directly impacts travel phase performance in remote operation mode.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system integrator. Cycle time KPI is a system-level outcome, not owned by NGHE Solution alone. Diffusion across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services is high. Not the right IM boundary for detailed cycle time optimization.

Vehicle owns the primary mechanisms for cycle time performance: drivetrain, hydraulics, control algorithms, and phase execution. Load, travel, and dump phases are Vehicle responsibilities. Interfaces with Safety Services and Teleoperation are well-defined. High containment, low diffusion. Best candidate.

Teleoperation is responsible for operator interface and remote command transmission, but not for Vehicle phase execution. Latency impact on travel phase is a secondary concern. Cycle time optimization is primarily a Vehicle responsibility. Low containment.

Safety Services provides constraints and interlocks, but does not own cycle time optimization. Safety overhead must be minimized but is not the primary driver of cycle time performance. Low containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Vehicle module is the primary owner of cycle time performance. It contains the mechanisms (drivetrain, hydraulics, controls) that directly execute load, travel, and dump phases. Cycle time is a measurable outcome of Vehicle subsystem performance under Tasking commands and Safety Services constraints. Telemetry interface enables monitoring and feedback. Containment score 0.85 reflects strong ownership with manageable external dependencies.
containment_risks: Risk: Site conditions (terrain, load variability) are external to Vehicle module but significantly impact cycle time. Mitigation: Duty cycle characterization and contingency margins in phase budgets.
Risk: Teleoperation latency (Network Link) impacts travel phase in remote operation. Mitigation: Explicit latency budget allocation and network characterization.
Risk: Safety Services interlocks may introduce blocking delays if not carefully designed. Mitigation: Asynchronous safety design and non-blocking interlock architecture.
Risk: Operator skill and worksite policies introduce variability. Mitigation: Operator training, real-time feedback, and adaptive control strategies.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify Vehicle module scope to explicitly include control algorithms and optimization logic for phase execution. Define clear interfaces for Tasking (input), Telemetry (output), SafetyAlert (inout), and Network Link (inout). Establish phase budget allocation as a Vehicle design parameter, with contingency margins for site variability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-2 directly addresses FR-2 by decomposing cycle time into phase budgets and optimizing Vehicle subsystems to meet those budgets. The DP includes telemetry monitoring, adaptive feedback, and safety integration. No fundamental mismatch between requirement and solution concept. Coupling risks are identified and manageable.

diffusion_risks: Cycle time performance depends on Vehicle subsystems (drivetrain, hydraulics, controls), which are contained within Vehicle module. Low diffusion risk.
Teleoperation latency impact on travel phase is a secondary concern; primary responsibility is Vehicle execution. Manageable via Network Link interface.
Safety Services constraints are integrated via SafetyAlert interface; asynchronous design minimizes blocking risk. Manageable.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module has high containment (0.85) for cycle time optimization. Phase budgets, subsystem optimization, and telemetry monitoring are Vehicle responsibilities. External dependencies (site conditions, operator skill, Safety Services constraints) are managed via interfaces and contingency margins.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is measurable via Telemetry interface. Field trials under representative duty cycle conditions can validate p95 performance. Instrumentation of phase durations (load_time, travel_time, dump_time) enables root cause analysis and optimization feedback.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time requirement is allocated to Vehicle module with clear phase budget decomposition. Sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.5) provide allocation clarity to load, travel, dump phases and external dependencies (Teleoperation latency, Safety Services overhead). Ownership and interfaces are well-defined.

recommended_revisions: Refine phase budget allocation to include contingency margins (10-15%) for site variability and operator skill variance.
Establish explicit latency budget for Teleoperation Network Link to ensure travel phase performance in remote operation.
Define Safety Services asynchronous design requirements to prevent blocking of cycle time critical paths.
Specify telemetry data collection and real-time operator feedback mechanisms for cycle time monitoring and adaptive control.
Establish duty cycle baseline and site condition characterization as a prerequisite for field validation.

decision.justification: FR-2 (Cycle time KPI) is not atomic and requires further decomposition into phase-level functional requirements (load, travel, dump) and external dependency requirements (Teleoperation latency, Safety Services overhead). The selected DP-2 and assigned Vehicle module provide a sound foundation for the next level of decomposition. No fundamental design flaws or coupling issues require revision at the current level. Proceed to decompose sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.5) to the next level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The Vehicle shall execute load phase within load_time_budget under representative site conditions and load variability
FR-2.2: The Vehicle shall execute travel phase within travel_time_budget, accounting for terrain variability and Teleoperation latency in remote operation
FR-2.3: The Vehicle shall execute dump phase within dump_time_budget under representative site conditions
FR-2.4: The Teleoperation system shall not introduce latency exceeding latency_budget during remote operation, maintaining travel phase performance
FR-2.5: The Safety Services shall not impose cycle time penalties exceeding safety_overhead_budget through blocking interlocks or constraints

assumptions: Vehicle subsystems (drivetrain, hydraulics, controls) can be optimized to meet allocated phase budgets without major redesign.
Safety Services can be implemented asynchronously without compromising safety criticality.
Site conditions (terrain, load variability) can be characterized and accounted for in phase budget contingency margins.
Teleoperation latency is predictable and can be budgeted within travel phase time allocation.
Operator skill and worksite policies can be standardized through training and operational procedures.
open_risks: Site conditions may exceed baseline assumptions, causing p95 cycle time to exceed CT_target. Mitigation: Duty cycle characterization and contingency margins.
Teleoperation latency may be unpredictable in field conditions. Mitigation: Network characterization and adaptive control strategies.
Safety Services interlocks may introduce unexpected blocking delays. Mitigation: Asynchronous design and non-blocking architecture.
Operator skill variance may introduce cycle time variability. Mitigation: Training, real-time feedback, and adaptive control.
Phase budget allocation may be infeasible for one or more phases. Mitigation: Iterative refinement and subsystem optimization.
evidence_needed: Duty cycle baseline characterization (terrain, load variability, site conditions)
Vehicle subsystem performance data (drivetrain, hydraulics, control response times)
Teleoperation network latency measurements under field conditions
Safety Services interlock design and timing analysis
Operator skill assessment and training effectiveness data
Field trial results validating p95 cycle time performance against CT_target
